audiences.name: Marketing agency owners
audiences.whyTheyMatter: Small agency owners (under 20 employees) are drowning in client work while under-resourced internally. They understand the value of leverage but haven't operationalized AI inside their own business. A single engagement can produce recurring automation revenue and referrals across their network.
audiences.angle: Lead with ops leverage, not AI hype. The frame is: what makes your client work worth buying should not be the thing you sacrifice to scale. Automation protects the craft, it doesn't replace it.
audiences.automationHook: 5-minute follow-up automation: meeting transcript goes to Claude, gets processed, lands as a draft in their Gmail. Low lift to set up, immediately visible value. Offered as the consult hook.
audiences.ctaModel: Offer to set them up with the follow-up automation on a brief call. The call is the filter, not the pitch.

audiences.name: Fractional executives
audiences.whyTheyMatter: Fractional execs are selling time across multiple clients simultaneously. They need portable infrastructure that travels with them and compounds across engagements. A second brain and automated follow-up is a direct multiplier on what they charge.
audiences.angle: Expand leverage across more clients without hiring a team. The pitch is about what they can charge and deliver at scale, not just internal efficiency.
audiences.automationHook: 5-minute follow-up automation: same transcript-to-draft setup, but the angle is that it works across every client, every engagement, without context loss.
audiences.ctaModel: Offer to set them up with the follow-up automation on a brief call. The call is the filter, not the pitch.

audiences.name: Boutique advisors
audiences.whyTheyMatter: Boutique advisors have built something valuable over years and are hitting a ceiling on how much they can compound it. They are often aware of AI but skeptical of vibe coding as infrastructure. ReRev bridges the gap between knowing it exists and having it actually work.
audiences.angle: Compound what you have built beyond what vibe coding alone can stabilize. The frame is durability and depth, not speed.
audiences.automationHook: 5-minute follow-up automation as the entry point. Also strong candidates for the Claude-native second brain (semantic search over their full practice history).
audiences.ctaModel: Offer to set them up with the follow-up automation on a brief call. The call is the filter, not the pitch.

audiences.name: Interim leaders and nonprofit executives
audiences.whyTheyMatter: Interim leaders step into orgs with no ramp time and need to move fast with limited context. Portable infrastructure that travels with them across engagements is a direct professional differentiator. Nonprofit execs are chronically under-resourced and often can't hire ops support.
audiences.angle: Portable infrastructure that travels with you to every new engagement. The pitch is about showing up ready, not about AI in the abstract.
audiences.automationHook: 5-minute follow-up automation: especially powerful for interim leaders who are in back-to-back stakeholder meetings from day one and need follow-through without adding hours.
audiences.ctaModel: Offer to set them up with the follow-up automation on a brief call. The call is the filter, not the pitch.
